stimulus:
[Bar graph: Energy Density of Four Heating Fuels (in MJ/L).]

  • natural gas (liquefied): 22 MJ/L
  • kerosene: 36 MJ/L
  • heating oil: 38 MJ/L
  • biodiesel: 32 MJ/L

According to the graph, the fuel with the highest energy density is ______
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to complete the sentence?
rationale:
Choice A is correct because heating oil has the highest energy density on the graph at 38 MJ/L.

Choice B is incorrect: kerosene is 36 MJ/L, lower than heating oil. Choice C is incorrect: biodiesel is 32 MJ/L. Choice D is incorrect: natural gas (liquefied) is 22 MJ/L, the lowest of the four.
